claim: TCF7L2 variants are consistently associated with type 2 diabetes risk across multiple, genetically diverse human populations.

claim: TCF7L2 is the strongest human genetic locus for type 2 diabetes risk.

claim: In cohort studies (Finnish Diabetes Prevention Study, 2007; French Epidemiological Study on the Insulin Resistance Syndrome, 2011), TCF7L2 risk variants predicted future type 2 diabetes onset.
